children.heading: .—Contracts and cooperative agreements
children.label: 1 .—Contracts and cooperative agreements
children.text: The Secretary of the Army may enter into contracts and cooperative agreements with the Army West Point Athletic Association for the purpose of supporting the athletic programs of the Academy. Notwithstanding , the Secretary may enter such contracts or cooperative agreements on a sole source basis pursuant to . Notwithstanding chapter 63 of title 31, a cooperative agreement under this section may be used to acquire property or services for the direct benefit or use of the Academy.section 3201(e) of this titlesection 3204(a)(5) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Before entering into a contract or cooperative agreement under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall ensure that such contract or agreement includes appropriate financial controls to account for Academy and Association resources in accordance with accepted accounting principles.

children.label: i
children.text: are consistent with the terms of the contract or cooperative agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: will not compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Department of the Army.

children.heading: .—Leases
children.label: 3 .—Leases
children.text: shall not apply to any leases the Secretary may enter into with the Association for the purpose of supporting the athletic programs of the Academy.Section 2667(h) of this title

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: 1 .—Authority
children.text: To the extent required by a contract or cooperative agreement under subsection (a), the Secretary may provide support services to the Association while the Association conducts its support activities at the Academy. The Secretary may provide support services described in paragraph (2) only if the Secretary determines that the provision of such services is essential for the support of the athletic programs of the Academy.

children.label: A
children.text: In this subsection, the term “support services” includes utilities, office furnishings and equipment, communications services, records staging and archiving, audio and video support, and security systems in conjunction with the leasing or licensing of property.

children.label: i
children.text: housing for Association personnel on United States Army Garrison, West Point, New York; and

children.label: ii
children.text: enrollment of dependents of Association personnel in elementary and secondary schools under the same criteria applied to dependents of Federal employees under , except that educational services provided pursuant to this clause shall be provided on a reimbursable basis.section 2164(a) of this title

children.heading: .—No liability of the united states
children.label: 3 .—No liability of the united states
children.text: Any such support services may only be provided without any liability of the United States to the Association.

children.heading: .—Support received from the association
children.label: 1 .—Support received from the association
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Secretary may accept from the Association funds, supplies, and services for the support of the athletic programs of the Academy. For the purposes of this section, employees or personnel of the Association may not be considered to be employees of the United States.section 1342 of title 31

children.heading: .—Funds received from ncaa
children.label: 2 .—Funds received from ncaa
children.text: The Secretary may accept funds from the National Collegiate Athletic Association to support the athletic programs of the Academy.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 3 .—Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that contributions under this subsection and expenditure of funds pursuant to subsection (e) do not reflect unfavorably on the ability of the Department of the Army, any of its employees, or any member of the armed forces to carry out any responsibility or duty in a fair and objective manner, or compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Department of the Army, or any individual involved in such a program.

children.heading: .—Licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements
children.label: 1 .—Licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements
children.text: An agreement under subsection (a) may, consistent with (other than subsection (d) of such section), authorize the Association to enter into licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements relating to trademarks and service marks identifying the Academy, subject to the approval of the Secretary of the Army.section 2260 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: such agreement would reflect unfavorably on the ability of the Department of the Army, any of its employees, or any member of the armed forces to carry out any responsibility or duty in a fair and objective manner; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary determines that the use of the trademark or service mark would compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Department of the Army, or any individual involved in such a program.

children.heading: .—Retention and Use of Funds
children.label: e .—Retention and Use of Funds
children.text: Any funds received by the Secretary under this section may be retained for use in support of the athletic programs of the Academy and shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: .—Service on Association Board of Directors
children.label: f .—Service on Association Board of Directors
children.text: The Association is a designated entity for which authorization under sections 1033(a) and 1589(a) of this title may be provided.

to qualify as a nonprofit organization under section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and operates in accordance with this section, the law of the State of New York, and the constitution and bylaws of the Association; and
to operate exclusively to support the athletic programs of the Academy.

children.heading: .—Association Defined
children.label: h .—Association Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “Association” means the Army West Point Athletic Association.
